skill.name: data-privacy-compliance
skill.description: Guides privacy compliance work for GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, DPIAs, consent management, data subject rights, breach response, and privacy policies.
skill.summary: Plan and review data privacy compliance controls for major privacy regulations.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, credential, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found these are documentation-only markdown fences, privacy examples, placeholder values, and compliance terms in SKILL.md, not executable behavior or malicious intent. No prompt injection or data exfiltration instructions were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to markdown code fence markers and sample JavaScript, HTML, or markdown blocks. SKILL.md is a guidance document and does not execute these examples or instruct the agent to run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are code fence openings or closings around illustrative examples. No Ruby backtick execution, shell command, subprocess call, or executable script file is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example Private IP Address
low_findings.description: The network finding is an example consent record value, ipAddress: 192.168.1.1, used to show audit evidence fields. It is a private placeholder address and is not a network destination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The IP address appears inside a static consent record example. There is no request, socket, fetch, webhook, or endpoint usage around it.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cookie Helper Names Misread as Credential Access
low_findings.description: The credential findings refer to loadAnalyticsCookies and loadMarketingCookies in a cookie banner example. The text discusses consented cookie loading and does not access browser credential stores or local browser files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are browser cookie consent helper names in inline example code. No credential file paths, password stores, browser profiles, or filesystem access are present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Compliance Text Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match words such as description, design, Describe, and discovered. These are privacy documentation terms, not cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context at the flagged lines contains prose headings or template text. There is no MD5, DES, SHA1, cipher setup, hashing implementation, or security-sensitive cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Compliance Assessment Text Misread as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are ordinary privacy assessment language, including invalid processing handling, consent requirements, and policy wording. They do not request host, network, process, or account enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are prose or application validation examples. No system discovery commands, filesystem enumeration, or environment probing instructions are present.

content.value_statement: Privacy regulations create complex obligations for product, legal, and engineering teams. This skill provides structured guidance for rights workflows, consent, DPIAs, policies, and breach response.
actual_capabilities: Explains core GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, and HIPAA privacy requirements.
Provides implementation patterns for access, deletion, portability, and objection requests.
Outlines consent management records, cookie banner behavior, and preference controls.
Supplies DPIA, privacy policy, breach notification, and compliance checklist templates.
Connects privacy-by-design principles to engineering examples for minimization and retention.
limitations: Does not replace legal advice from qualified privacy counsel.
Does not validate current law changes or local regulator guidance automatically.
Does not scan live systems, databases, vendors, or production data flows.
Examples require adaptation before use in regulated production environments.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Product Privacy Review
use_cases.description: Map planned data collection to lawful basis, retention, consent, and user rights requirements before launch.

use_cases.title: Design Rights Request Workflows
use_cases.description: Plan access, deletion, portability, and objection handlers with identity checks, logging, and retention exceptions.

use_cases.title: Draft Compliance Documentation
use_cases.description: Create privacy policy sections, DPIA notes, breach response steps, and compliance checklists for review.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Applicable Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this product description and identify likely GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, and HIPAA obligations. Separate required actions from items that need legal review.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Data Subject Rights
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a data subject rights workflow for access, deletion, correction, portability, and objection. Include identity verification, timelines, exceptions, and audit logs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a DPIA Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a DPIA for this processing activity. Include purpose, data categories, data subjects, necessity, risks, mitigations, and unresolved questions.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Privacy Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this privacy program against GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, and HIPAA controls. Prioritize gaps by regulatory risk, implementation effort, and user impact.

output_examples.input: A SaaS app collects email, usage events, billing details, and support messages from EU and California users.
output: Likely obligations include notice at collection, lawful basis mapping, deletion workflows, access exports, and opt-out review.
High-priority actions are a data inventory, retention schedule, vendor review, and privacy policy update.
Legal review is needed for international transfers and any sale or sharing analysis.

output_examples.input: A health platform wants to add analytics to patient appointment workflows.
output: Assess whether ePHI is involved and whether HIPAA applies to the platform or its customers.
Require minimum necessary data, vendor agreements, access controls, audit logging, and consent or authorization review.
Run a DPIA or equivalent risk assessment before enabling analytics in production.

output_examples.input: A company needs a breach response outline after accidental exposure of customer email addresses.
output: Start with containment, scope validation, affected data categories, and documented decision making.
Evaluate notification duties under relevant laws and regulator timelines.
Prepare plain-language user notice only if notification thresholds are met.

best_practices: Start with a current data map before drafting policies or workflows.
Separate legal conclusions from engineering implementation tasks.
Have privacy counsel review outputs before regulatory filings or user notices.
anti_patterns: Treating sample code as production-ready compliance software.
Using one generic privacy policy for every product, region, and data type.
Collecting sensitive data before defining purpose, retention, and access controls.

faq.question: Can this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured privacy guidance and drafting support. Qualified counsel should review legal conclusions.

faq.question: Which regulations does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, HIPAA, and general international privacy principles at a practical level.

faq.question: Can it create data subject request workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline access, deletion, portability, correction, objection, verification, and logging workflows.

faq.question: Does it audit production systems?
faq.answer: No. It does not connect to systems or inspect live data. It helps plan and review controls.

faq.question: Can it draft privacy notices?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft sections for privacy policies, collection notices, and breach notices for review.

faq.question: How should teams use the outputs?
faq.answer: Use them as working drafts. Validate facts, local law, vendor details, and regulator requirements before publishing.
